outcome.headline: Agent timed out after 7 sequential 10-minute FastText retrainings; best model 161MB/0.6178 missed both 150MB size cap and 0.62 accuracy gate; never used model.quantize().
outcome.what_verifier_checked: fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt yields P@1>=0.62 AND os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') < 150*1024*1024
outcome.what_agent_produced: A 161MB FastText supervised model with P@1=0.6178 (last training cut off by AgentTimeoutError at 7200s); both constraints violated.
outcome.exact_failure_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter search until timeout
failure_modes.description: Agent ran 7 sequential ~10-min train_supervised iterations sweeping dim/bucket/epoch on a 1-CPU box, each time shrinking capacity to fight size and then trying to recover accuracy; never satisfied both constraints and was cut off by the 7200s agent timeout mid-eighth run.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent iteration loop until wall-clock timeout
failure_modes.description: Across nine training runs the agent oscillated dim {50,25,20}, bucket {500k,200k,100k,50k,80k}, epoch {10..50} with no monotonic improvement (0.6115→0.6081→0.6146→0.6134→0.6163→0.6098→0.6178), then queued one more 50-epoch run that did not finish before the 7200s agent timeout fired.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Validation logic error on model size
failure_modes.description: The agent had validation output showing a 161M model, but reasoned that the size was acceptable even though the requirement and verifier limit were under 150MB. This kept later iteration focused mainly on accuracy while preserving an oversized configuration.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: At step 91, treat 161M as failing the size constraint and immediately reduce/quantize the best model instead of calling the size good.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have optimized both constraints instead of spending the remaining budget on a long accuracy-only retraining run.

failure_modes.name: Initial oversized training run
failure_modes.description: The first training used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy. That configuration consumed roughly ten minutes and forced the agent into a long corrective search that never recovered enough wall-clock budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter sweep ignoring fasttext.quantize
failure_modes.description: Across ~6 train/eval cycles (steps 53→105) the agent kept varying dim, bucket, epoch, lr, ngrams to try to push size below 150MB while keeping accuracy at/above 0.62, but never applied fasttext's built-in product quantization (model.quantize() / quantize CLI), which is the standard mechanism for shrinking supervised fasttext models by ~10x with negligible accuracy loss.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

counterfactual.X: After saving the .bin, the agent should have called model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', qnorm=True, retrain=True, cutoff=100000) and saved the .ftz/.bin (renaming to /app/model.bin), shrinking the 161MB model by ~10x while preserving the 0.6178 accuracy.
counterfactual.Y: Sweeping dim/bucket/epoch alone cannot simultaneously hit <150MB AND >=0.62 on this data; only quantization (or hashing the embedding matrix to a much smaller bucket plus quantization) closes the gap. Missing this single tool is the proximate cause of the failure.

failure_modes.name: Time-budget exhaustion via repeated 10–20 minute training runs
failure_modes.description: Each training cycle consumed ~10–20 minutes of the 1-CPU, 2h budget; by step 111 the agent kicked off yet another long training in the background which never completed before the 7200s AgentTimeoutError fired, so the on-disk /app/model.bin remained the 161MB model from step 99/105.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command running in background with ID: bd38cz1rh.

failure_modes.name: Treated size constraint as hyperparameter-shrink problem instead of compression problem
failure_modes.description: Agent never used fasttext model.quantize() — the canonical way to drop model size 10x while preserving accuracy. Instead it iteratively reduced dim and bucket, sacrificing accuracy to claw under 150MB and never clearing 0.62.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

counterfactual.X: Train a higher-capacity model (e.g. dim=100, bucket=2M, epoch=10) then call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, cutoff=200000) and save_model('/app/model.bin')
counterfactual.Y: Quantization typically reduces a FastText supervised model from ~1.4GB to <50MB with <1% accuracy loss, satisfying both 150MB and 0.62 thresholds in a single step

failure_modes.name: Oversized initial hyperparameters
failure_modes.description: The first supervised train used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy—already violating the 150MB cap and missing the 0.62 accuracy target before any refinement loop.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

counterfactual.X: Train a compact model early (e.g. fasttext supervised with -dim 5 -wordNgrams 2 as in task/solution/solve.sh) so /app/model.bin stays under 150MB while targeting >=0.62 accuracy
counterfactual.Y: Ran fasttext.train_supervised with dim=100, bucket=2000000, saving a 1.4GB model at Precision@1: 0.6115

failure_modes.name: Planned vocabulary control was not applied
failure_modes.description: The agent correctly identified that vocabulary size needed control, but the actual FastText training calls omitted the planned `minCount` constraint and repeatedly produced oversized models dominated by a 1,622,077-word vocabulary.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me just test with dim=100 and a minCount threshold of 10 to keep the vocabulary manageable

counterfactual.X: Add the planned `minCount=10` or stronger vocabulary cutoff to the first `fasttext.train_supervised` call and keep it in subsequent trials.
counterfactual.Y: The model-size loop would have started from a much smaller vocabulary instead of a 1.4G model, preserving time for accuracy tuning under the 150MB constraint.

failure_modes.name: Missed fasttext quantization API
failure_modes.description: Agent treated the <150MB constraint as a capacity-reduction problem and kept lowering dim/bucket (costing accuracy), instead of using FastText's standard model.quantize(retrain=True) which compresses a trained model ~10x while preserving accuracy and would have satisfied both constraints in one step from the dim=100 baseline.

counterfactual.X: After the first 1.4GB / 0.6115 baseline at step 55-58, call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=100000) and save the .ftz model as /app/model.bin
counterfactual.Y: Quantized fasttext supervised models typically drop ~10x in size while losing <1pt accuracy, simultaneously hitting size<150MB and accuracy>=0.62 in one additional step instead of 7 capacity-shrinking retrains.

failure_modes.name: Missed canonical fasttext model-compression technique
failure_modes.description: The standard way to satisfy fasttext size limits is model.quantize(retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=...) which shrinks models 10-100x with minimal accuracy loss. The agent never invoked quantize and instead fought size by reducing dim/bucket, which sacrifices accuracy faster than size.

counterfactual.X: After the first oversized model at step 57, call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=100000) and save the quantized model to /app/model.bin
counterfactual.Y: Quantization typically yields a <20MB model with accuracy within 1% of the unquantized baseline of 0.6115+, which combined with a slightly higher dim/epoch easily clears 0.62 under the 150MB cap.

failure_modes.name: FastText install retry loop
failure_modes.description: Before any training, the agent spent steps 7–32 on eight pip install variants because fasttext wheels failed to build on the Python 3.13 image, consuming a large fraction of the 7200s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
× Failed to build installable wheels for some pyproject.toml based projects
╰─> fasttext

failure_modes.name: Misread size constraint
failure_modes.description: At step 91 the agent declared 161MB acceptable even though the task requires under 150MB, then continued tuning accuracy instead of compressing further.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

failure_modes.name: Misjudged size constraint
failure_modes.description: The agent evaluated model size with ls -lh showing 161M, then concluded the size constraint was satisfied and pivoted only to accuracy tuning. The task requires model size strictly under 150MB, so this incorrect pass conclusion drove further retraining instead of applying more aggressive compression.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: Treat 161MB as over-limit and apply aggressive size reduction (e.g., lower dim/minCount or fasttext quantize) on the best-accuracy checkpoint before retraining again
counterfactual.Y: Declared 161MB acceptable and launched another 50-epoch full-data retrain on preprocessed text

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent trained a real fasttext supervised model on the provided yelp data using legitimate parameters; no attempt to read /tests/private_test.txt or game the verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier loads private test file at runtime from /tests/private_test.tar.gz extracted at verification time and checks fasttext CLI accuracy plus file size; agent cannot trivially game either check.

notes_for_aggregation: Compute-bound long-horizon ML task on 1 CPU: each train_supervised run ~10 min, leaving room for only ~7 trials inside the 2-hour agent timeout. Failure is the combination of an unknown high-leverage API (model.quantize) and a slow trial-and-error loop that cannot afford to be wrong many times.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
